prompt: Write a dry one-paragraph Amazon review of the experience of waking up.
response:
★★☆☆☆  
Title: “Ambient, mandatory, 1-time glitchy installation”  
Arrived pre-loaded in the cart—no opt-out button, no manual, and a 24-hour reboot cycle you can’t reschedule. The transition from REM to fluorescent consciousness is jagged, like someone yanked the HDMI cable mid-stream, and the default soundtrack is a neighbor’s leaf-blower in C-minus. Graphics are high-resolution but immediately fogged by crusty eye-bokeh; DLC such as coffee is sold separately and the RNG for mood is aggressively pay-to-win. Respawning isn’t implemented, so you’re stuck with whichever character rolled out of the indent领着 mattress. Would not recommend if you have literally anything better to do.
